content: The Next Web https://thenextweb.com Original and proudly opinionated perspectives for Generation T Thu, 16 Jul 2026 19:18:18 +0000 en-US hourly 1 TNW-API Visa launched a stablecoin platform that sent Circle shares tumbling six percent https://thenextweb.com/news/visa-stablecoin-platform-open-usd Thu, 16 Jul 2026 18:59:57 +0000 TheNextWeb=3843538aec7e5167ea4f3e2c5a95c3eb Visa launched the Visa Stablecoin Platform on Thursday, a new infrastructure product that lets banks and payment processors issue, manage, and settle stablecoin transactions through Visa’s network. The platform launches with native support for Open USD, the stablecoin backed by a consortium of more than 140 firms including Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, and BlackRock. Jack Forestell, […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> The EU is forcing Google to open Android to rival AI and share its search data https://thenextweb.com/news/eu-google-android-ai-search-data-digital-markets-act Thu, 16 Jul 2026 18:16:53 +0000 TheNextWeb=1ef564c8855f5fe2bca71d84a5f3cfcc Google will have to let rival AI assistants run on Android the way its own Gemini does. It must also hand some of its search data to competitors. The European Commission set out both requirements on Thursday. The two decisions fall under the EU’s Digital Markets Act (DMA), the Commission said. The law forces “gatekeeper” […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> AI and a brain implant restored a paralysed man’s movement and touch https://thenextweb.com/news/feinstein-double-neural-bypass-bci-paralysis-nature-medicine Thu, 16 Jul 2026 17:55:47 +0000 TheNextWeb=e89c80caae4fbc05e24b9c8ceb674506 Researchers have restored hand movement and the sense of touch to a man paralysed from the chest down. The results, published in Nature Medicine, suggest the technology partly rewired his nervous system. The system, called a “double neural bypass,” comes from the Feinstein Institutes for Medical Research, the research arm of Northwell Health, the team […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Verizon cuts 3,000 retail jobs as AI replaces customer service and stores move to franchises https://thenextweb.com/news/verizon-3000-retail-layoffs-store-reorganization Thu, 16 Jul 2026 17:50:01 +0000 TheNextWeb=9f1b7d18d14479295337eb3e48a869d7 Verizon is cutting about 3,000 employees from its corporate-owned retail stores and transferring 274 locations to independent operators, Bloomberg reported on Thursday. The changes take effect on August 16 and will leave the largest US mobile carrier with 1,000 company-owned stores alongside roughly 5,000 independent franchises. A company spokesperson said many of the workers at […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> 29 countries sign a treaty to establish the World AI Cooperation Organization https://thenextweb.com/news/china-world-ai-cooperation-organization-waico-signed Thu, 16 Jul 2026 17:28:47 +0000 TheNextWeb=4ede0b440ac1d6d2a1320be75732277a Twenty-nine countries signed an agreement on 16 July to establish the World AI Cooperation Organization (WAICO), Reuters reported. China says the intergovernmental body aims to promote international cooperation and global governance in AI. China’s foreign minister, Wang Yi, signed on behalf of Beijing. UN Secretary-General António Guterres attended the ceremony. The founding members include Russia, […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Google renames NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook and expands code execution to Pro users https://thenextweb.com/news/google-notebooklm-gemini-notebook-rebrand Thu, 16 Jul 2026 17:06:03 +0000 TheNextWeb=ddeb39166d9fcbf24f4f11676ca16408 Google is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, folding one of its most popular AI products into the Gemini brand while keeping it as a standalone tool. The company said on Thursday that the rebrand reflects how deeply the research assistant has been woven into the broader Google ecosystem, including the Gemini app and Google Search. […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> SWISSto12 raised $70M, and it is that rare thing: a profitable space startup https://thenextweb.com/news/swissto12-70m-series-c-sovereign-space-infrastructure Thu, 16 Jul 2026 16:48:40 +0000 TheNextWeb=e0befebfda5257117c8adc1171087bff Most space-hardware startups raise money because they are burning it. SWISSto12 raised $70m while turning a profit, an unusual thing in the satellite business. The Swiss company closed the €61m round days after ESA member states put $84.8m into its HummingSat programme, it said. That is more than $150m of fresh capital in a month. The coupe-SUV is built around the company’s proprietary Turing AI chips, with every trim shipping at least one and the top Ultra variant carrying three for a combined 2,250 trillion operations per second. It is the […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Fireworks raised $1.5bn on a bet that companies will build AI, not rent it https://thenextweb.com/news/fireworks-1-5-billion-series-d-specialized-intelligence Thu, 16 Jul 2026 16:30:35 +0000 TheNextWeb=690c019cbb42195258b771cfceba298e The AI economy has run on a simple habit: rent a model from a big lab. Fireworks just raised $1.5bn to argue the opposite, that every company will build intelligence of its own. The Series D values the San Mateo startup at $17.5bn, it said. Atreides Management, Index Ventures, and TCV led the round.
